content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886


November 27, 2001


Dave Todd
1487 No. Farris Avenue
Fresno, CA 93728-1521

Re:     Your Request for Advice
        Our File No.  A-01-258

Dear Mr. Todd:

       This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the gift provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ This letter is limited solely to provisions of the
Act, and should not be taken as advice or an opinion regarding any other areas of the law
potentially raised by your letter. This advice is based upon the facts as provided in your
request letter and in subsequent telephone conversations with you.² Please note that this
letter should not be construed to evaluate any conduct that has already taken place.
(Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).)

QUESTION

       As a water conservation supervisor with the City of Fresno, may you receive a
discount on a tankless water heater that was offered to you by the manufacturer at an
installation seminar that you attended?

CONCLUSION

       You may receive the discount on the water heater because it is a discount that was
offered "to members of the public without regard to official status."


___________________________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
² This advice is applicable and confers immunity only to the extent that the facts provided to us are
correct, and that all of the material facts have been disclosed. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71;
§ 83114.)

File No. A-01-258
Page No. 2

FACTS

You are currently employed as a water conservation supervisor for the City of
Fresno. You recently attended a certification meeting (to train tankless hot water heater
installers) sponsored by Rinnai, a manufacturer of tankless water heaters. The fliers
advertising the meeting were available at the local distributor of the product. This
distributor serves a clientele of professional plumbers and air conditioning service
persons. 3 The target audience for the flier was the distributor's clientele. However, upon
request, any member of the public could have attended.

The flier for the class stated in part as follows:

• Who should Attend:
• Plumbers, Builders, HVAC Contractors, Inspectors…
• Topics
• Proper Installation and applications
• Troubleshooting
• Repair Techniques
• Hydronic Heating applications
• Residential & Commercial applications…

The purpose of your attendance was to gather information for a generic report on hot
water heaters for the mayor and for a grant proposal that you are preparing for the U.S.
Bureau of Reclamation. You became aware of the class in a conversation with the
manufacturer regarding your research when he suggested that attendance at the class
would be a good way for you to gain more knowledge about the issue.

After the certification meeting, the participants were notified that they were
eligible to purchase one tankless water heater for personal use at a 50% discount if they
did so within 30 days. You would like to buy one.

ANALYSIS

The Act provides that no elected state officer, elected officer of a local
government agency, or other individual specified in section 87200 shall accept gifts from
any single source in a calendar year with a total value of more than $320. (Section
89503(a).) A designated employee of a local government agency is prohibited from
accepting a gift from any single source in any calendar year with a total value of more
than $320 "if the member or employee would be required to report the receipt of income
or gifts from that source on his or her statement of economic interests."
(Section 89503(c).)

_______________
3 In telephone conversations with you, additional information as to the nature of the distributor's
business, the class being open to the public, and the circumstances of your attendance was obtained.

File No. A-01-258
Page No. 3

The definition of a gift includes a discount on the price of goods unless the "discount is made in the regular course of business to members of the public without regard to official status." Under the facts provided, the discount appears to have been offered to members of the public "without regard to official status." Presumably, the manufacturer's notification that you were eligible to purchase one tankless water heater for personal use at a discount is a standard business practice.4 Moreover, while it would appear that the class in question was advertised only to professional plumbers and air conditioning servicepersons, any member of the public could have attended. As such, the discount was made available to you as a member of the public without regard to your official status, and would not be considered a gift under the Act. (Francis Advice Letter, No. A-00-231.)

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: William L. Williams, Jr.
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

WLW:jg
I:\AdviceLetrs\01-258

4 If these are not the facts, you should seek further written advice because our advice may differ.
